title: Common-Size & Trend Percents
event: Reyes Co.'s sales were $200,000 in the base year and $250,000 in the current year. Current-year cost of goods sold is $150,000. Compute the sales trend percent and the common-size percent for COGS.

computationPaths.narration: Trend percents compare a line to its own BASE-YEAR value; common-size compares a line to the SAME year's anchor (sales).

memorize.body: Trend % = current ÷ base × 100. Common-size % = line item ÷ that year's net sales (or total assets).
Trend of 125% means sales are 25% ABOVE base — read it as a level, not a change.

distractors.feedback: That's the CHANGE. Trend percents express the current level as a % of base: 250 ÷ 200 = 125%.
You inverted the fraction — base over current. Current year goes on top.
